summary: PM/CRM full parity (iteration 17) verified. Backend: 14/14 new parity tests + 35/35 previous CRM tests green. CRM medicine/appointment/lab-booking endpoints correctly dual-write to shared db.medications/db.appointments and CRM additions are visible via the main PM endpoints (proves mobile/customer app will see them). New /api/crm/onboarding/pending endpoint works and is auth-gated. Frontend: Onboarding card + filter-onboarding tab + onboarding-task-* items render correctly on /crm; PM Dashboard no longer shows FlaskConical/Lab Test button on user rows; PM Lab Test Booking modal has been removed. All 4 CRM smoke pages load without webpack overlay or console errors.

minor.issue: Default limit=5 silently hides older test data. Minor — callers must pass ?limit= if they need more than 5. Documenting for future reference.

issues: Recharts console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' — chart container has no explicit minWidth/minHeight, cosmetic only

action_items: (Cosmetic) Fix Recharts width/height warning on CRM dashboard by adding minWidth/minHeight to chart container or giving the ResponsiveContainer a defined parent size.
(Optional polish) Add a minor Recharts ResponsiveContainer wrapper fix to silence the -1 width/height warning.
critical_code_review_comments: crm.py lines 962-1020 (add_medicine): correctly reuses main app Medication pydantic model + calls generate_adherence_logs_for_medication — strong parity guarantee.

crm.py lines 1324-1396 (book_lab_test): clean dual-write pattern — writes to crm_lab_bookings and mirrors into db.appointments with source='crm_lab_booking'. OneSignal call is guarded by try/except so it will not fail the request if push creds are misconfigured. Good.
crm.py lines 689-720 (onboarding/pending): sort desc by created_at + 200 limit is sensible. Consider adding pagination if dataset grows.
PM appointments endpoint default limit=5 is tight; safe but surprising when tested from scripts.

success_rate.backend: 100% (49/49 = 35 existing CRM + 14 new parity; 1 skipped because PM adherence GET is POST-only)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all required UI assertions pass: onboarding card, filter tab, tab render, PM lab removal, 4 smoke pages load cleanly)

test_credentials: PM session_token=test_pm_crm_2026 (diabexpertonline@gmail.com, id 2069063a-50c1-4f99-99e5-5b5d2b21d7ab). Customer id f2eefadb-a488-495f-b93f-09300ceb112a (Sarun Kumar / advsarun@gmail.com). Both already pre-seeded in db.sessions.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed medications, appointments and lab bookings during run; medication is deleted in delete test, appointments/lab bookings persist (by design) but are clearly labeled TEST_<uuid> and do not interfere with production flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 17 parity tests are stable. If backend changes happen to Medication or Appointment pydantic models, the test payloads in test_crm_pm_parity.py (TestMedicineParity.test_crm_add_medicine_writes_to_db_medications and TestAppointmentParity.test_crm_create_appointment_writes_shared) may need matching updates — in particular schedule.start_date is required and dosage_timings[].amount must be a STRING. PM appointments endpoint GET requires ?limit=50 (default 5) when asserting inclusion of newly-created apts. Frontend auth pattern from iteration 16 still works (add_init_script sets localStorage before first goto).
rca of the issue: No open issues. Both parity regressions raised during test authoring (1) Medication pydantic requires schedule.start_date + string amount, (2) PM GET /appointments default limit=5, were quickly understood via 500 response bodies and test payload/query param updates — they were test-setup issues, not code bugs.
